name: ULTIMATE CHILLI
description: Perhaps the ingredient list overextends the richest we've ever tasted. The flavour comes from the mushroom base, but is boosted by untraditional ingredients like soy sauce, balsamic vinegar, maple syrup and chocolate. You should absolutely make it bubbling away if you have the time - the flavours meld beautifully and you'll be bowled over by the end result. Turn the page for a mouthwatering preview.
method: Food processor | Frying pan on a medium-high heat | Large saucepan on a medium heat
Put the mushrooms in the food processor and pulse very finely - minced you can call it | Put them in a dish | Heat a generous slug of olive oil in a food processor

Pour a little oil into the hot frying pan | Once the oil is hot, add the mushrooms with the salt and pepper and cook for 5 minutes | Remove from the heat and cook gently, so they don't burn | Season well

Peel and mince the red onions | Peel and mince the garlic | Slice the tops from the chilies, halve them lengthways and scrape out all the seeds with a teaspoon (save them if you like it hotter) | Remove the leaves from the coriander and set aside | Finely chop the stalks | Remove the minced root from the celery | Cut the pepper in half, remove the stem and seeds | Cut the celery and pepper into very small chunks

Add a little oil to the large saucepan | Once it is hot, add the minced onions and garlic, the finely chopped coriander stalks and the minced chilli, cook gently for 10 minutes, making sure you stir occasionally in between | Add the chopped pepper and celery and cook for a further 10 minutes

Add all the spice mix | Sprinkle in the bay leaves and stir continuously for about a minute to coat all the vegetables | Stir in the tomato purée to give a rich colour and depth | Stir in the maple syrup, soy sauce, chocolate and balsamic for about 2 minutes | Pour in the vegetable stock and season generously with salt and pepper | Stir until everything has blended

Bring to the boil, then turn down the heat to high | Stir constantly until the liquid has reduced by two thirds and the sauce has thickened | Tip the beans from the tin, drained and add the mushrooms into the pan, let simmer for 5 minutes, until the sauce is thick

Drain and rinse the black beans under cold running water and add them to the pan along with the maple syrup, dark chocolate and the minced mushrooms | Mix everything together really well and then continue to cook gently, stirring occasionally and letting away with the lid off, stirring occasionally until it's reduced to the right thickness (at least 10 minutes, but possibly longer)
If bubbling for longer, you may need to deepen the flavours, adding more water if needed to keep the right consistency
Take the lid off the pan and remove the bay leaf | Stir the coriander leaves into the chili and serve - or make Big Bad Method
